content.problemStatement:
Declare two variables **x** and **y**, and assign 202020 to **x** and 666 to **y**.
Divide **x** by **y** (i.e., perform integer division **x // y**) and print the result.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer – the result of 202020 // 666.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 202020 divided by 666 equals 303 with remainder 252. Integer division discards the remainder, so the printed result is 303.

solution.approach: The program does not need to read any data. It simply stores the two constants x = 202020 and y = 666, computes the integer division x // y, and prints the result. To satisfy the requirement of handling multiple test cases, we define a constant T = 1 and repeat the computation T times.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – only a few arithmetic operations are performed.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a handful of primitive variables are used.
